essence: Inspects buildings and workplaces to ensure compliance with fire‑safety laws, preventing risks before emergencies occur.

identity.title: Fire Safety Inspector
identity.summary: Assesses fire risks, inspects buildings, enforces safety regulations, and advises organisations on fire‑prevention measures.

salary.notes: Senior inspectors, enforcement officers, and specialists in complex buildings can exceed £50k-£60k+.

qualifications: Fire safety or fire‑risk assessment training
Knowledge of fire‑safety legislation and building regulations
Ability to conduct inspections and write compliance reports
Understanding of fire‑prevention systems and evacuation planning

lifestyle.description: Fire safety inspectors visit buildings, workplaces, and public venues to assess fire risks, check compliance, and enforce regulations. They review evacuation plans, inspect fire‑safety systems, and advise organisations on improvements. The role suits individuals who enjoy investigative work, public safety, and ensuring safe environments.

habits: Conducts thorough inspections and risk assessments
Follows structured compliance and enforcement procedures
Keeps detailed inspection and incident records
Communicates clearly with building owners and managers
